scoring_breakdown.explanation: Score breakdown: Required fields (50/50), Description quality (25/25 for 99/100 score), Character limit (10/10), Missing optional-but-recommended fields (-10 for allowed-tools)

validator_notes: The ARD demonstrates EXCELLENT adherence to the Global Standard Skill Schema
The frontmatter example is minimal and clean (only name + description)
Description quality analysis is thorough and well-documented
The ARD correctly identifies allowed-tools in section 3.1 but doesn't include it in the frontmatter example
This appears to be an intentional choice to show minimal frontmatter, but recommended fields should be included
Overall, this is a high-quality ARD that closely follows the global standard
summary: The ARD contains a proper SKILL.md frontmatter example that includes both required fields (name, description) and achieves exceptional description quality (99/100). The frontmatter follows the minimal approach with only name and description fields. However, the ARD documents the need for 'allowed-tools' in section 3.1 but doesn't include it in the actual frontmatter example at lines 109-114. Adding the recommended optional fields (allowed-tools, version) would bring this to near-perfect compliance (95+ score).
